text: Stock Market Today: Dow, Nasdaq Open Lower; Oil Climbs — Live Updates - WSJ
fullText: Live updates on markets and the top finance, economics and business stories. Plus the latest on oil prices, the Dow, S&P 500 and Nasdaq.

text: White House Correspondents’ Dinner shooting suspect pleads not guilty in federal court - NBC News
fullText: The man charged with attempting to assassinate President Donald Trump at the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner last month pleaded not guilty at a Monday arraignment in federal court

text: Live Updates: Iran vows to fight on, oil prices surge as Trump rejects peace proposal as "unacceptable" - CBS News
fullText: Oil prices spike and the Strait of Hormuz stays paralyzed as Trump rejects Iran's peace proposal and Tehran vows to "fight whenever it is necessary."

text: April home sales disappoint as higher mortgage rates weigh on buyers - CNBC
fullText: Home sales barely moved in April, as mortgage rates shot higher the month before and uncertainty over the war with Iran weighed on consumers.

text: Missouri announces RB Ahmad Hardy shot at concert, in stable condition - On3

fullText: Tom Brady made a surprise appearance at "The Roast of Kevin Hart" on May 10 to get some payback. He even hit on Hart's wife in the audience.

text: Pentagon will review Senator Mark Kelly's comments about US weapon stockpiles, Hegseth says - BBC
fullText: The defence secretary accuses the senator of "blabbing on TV", after Kelly expressed concern about the US munitions stockpile.

text: Confident in China’s power, Xi is ready to host an unpredictable Trump - The Washington Post
fullText: Holding no illusions about making lasting deals at this week’s summit, China’s leader looks to project Beijing as an alternative to U.S. volatility on the world stage.

text: Iran hangs grad student accused of spying for the CIA and Israel's intel agency - CBS News
fullText: Erfan Shakourzadeh, 29, was hanged after being convicted for allegedly collaborating with the CIA and Israel's Mossad intelligence service, Iran's judiciary said.

text: Hantavirus cases spark surge in pharma and biotech stocks — here’s why - CNBC
fullText: The outbreak of Hantavirus on a cruise ship has led to biotech and pharmaceutical stocks surging on reports of firms developing vaccines.

text: Windows 11 is getting a macOS-like speed boost - The Verge
fullText: Microsoft has started testing a new speed boost feature for Windows 11. It dynamically adjusts CPU clocks to speed up menus, flyouts, apps, and more.

text: 6 bodies found in Union Pacific boxcar in Laredo, Texas, near Mexico, police say - CBS News
fullText: Six people were found dead in a Union Pacific Railroad boxcar in Laredo, Texas, at the Mexican border, police there said, without offering any details.

text: Three enjoyable ways to slow your brain's ageing - BBC
fullText: Our brains thrive on a challenge, but it doesn't all have to be hard work to see the health benefits. Here are three simple and fun ways to protect your brain as you age.

text: Nobel laureate Narges Mohammadi transferred to a Tehran hospital, her foundation says - NPR
fullText: Nobel Peace laureate and activist Narges Mohammadi has been transferred to a Tehran hospital more than a week after collapsing in prison, her foundation said Sunday.

text: The families going hungry because of Trump’s food stamp cuts - NBC News
fullText: Under President Donald Trump’s “big, beautiful bill,” food stamp applicants are fighting to prove eligibility. A crisis in Arizona offers a warning for America.
